title: Kundalini Triggered by Reiki: When Energy Healing Starts Something Bigger
meta_description: You're here, aren't you? Probably 3 AM, staring at the ceiling, heart pounding, mind racing. You had some Reiki, right? Thought it'd be chill, maybe help w
body:
Kundalini Triggered by Reiki: When Energy Healing Starts Something Bigger

You're here, aren't you? Probably 3 AM, staring at the ceiling, heart pounding, mind racing. You had some Reiki, right? Thought it'd be chill, maybe help with stress, clear some old gunk. And now... this. This thing that feels like your insides are vibrating, your brain's re-wiring itself, and you're pretty sure you're either going crazy or dying. Or both. Yeah, I know that feeling. I've been there. You're not alone, even if it feels like the loneliest place on earth right now.

I'm Kalesh. I went through my own kundalini crisis, triggered by a different energy practice, but the mechanics, the sheer terror, the feeling of being utterly lost - it's all the same. So, when you're reading this, know it's coming from someone who gets it. Someone who survived it. And you will too. It's not a quick fix, it's not a magic pill, but it's survivable. And honestly, it can even be a gift, eventually. But right now, it just feels like hell. And that's okay. We're gonna talk about what's happening, why it's happening, and what you can actually do about it.

The Unexpected Awakening: What Just Happened?

So, you went for some Reiki. Or maybe you practiced it yourself. And now you're experiencing a wild array of physical, emotional, mental, and even spiritual sensations that are completely outside your normal. You might be feeling intense heat or cold, tingling, buzzing, electric shocks. Maybe involuntary movements, tremors, spasms. Your sleep's probably shot to hell. You might be having bizarre dreams, or no dreams at all, just waking up feeling like you haven't slept a wink. Emotionally, you're a rollercoaster - sudden bursts of rage, uncontrollable crying, overwhelming fear, or even brief moments of ecstatic bliss that feel completely out of place. Mentally, it's a mess. Racing thoughts, difficulty concentrating, memory issues, a sense of derealization or depersonalization - like you're not quite real, or your body isn't yours. Spiritually, well, that's where it gets really weird. You might feel a connection to something vast and nameless, or a complete disconnection. You might be seeing things, hearing things, sensing things you never did before. It's a lot, right? It's too much.

What's likely happened is that the energy work - in this case, Reiki - activated your kundalini. Now, before you freak out even more, let's talk about what kundalini is. It's not some evil demon or a curse. It's an ancient concept, found in many spiritual traditions, describing a dormant spiritual energy believed to reside at the base of your spine. Think of it like a coiled serpent. When it "awakens" or "rises," it travels up the central energy channel (sushumna nadi) in your body, clearing blockages and initiating a deep transformation. Sounds beautiful and poetic, right? In theory. In practice, especially when it's unexpected or unbalanced, it can feel like a spiritual emergency. You're basically going through a spiritual detox, a re-wiring of your entire being, and your system isn't prepared for the intensity. It's like trying to run a supercomputer program on a 1980s PC. It's gonna crash and burn, or at least stutter violently.

Reiki, while generally gentle, works with life force energy. For some people, especially if you're already sensitive, have a history of trauma, or have an underlying spiritual predisposition, that energy can act as a trigger. It's not the Reiki's fault, and it's not your fault. It's just a confluence of factors that led to an unexpected and intense awakening. You didn't ask for this, but here you are. And it's real. No one can tell you it's not. Your experience is valid, and you're not imagining it.

"I Think I'm Losing My Mind": What to Do When It Feels Too Much

That feeling of losing your mind? It's common. It's terrifying. But you're not losing your mind in the traditional sense of mental illness, though it can certainly feel like it. You're experiencing a shift in consciousness, and your ego, your sense of self, is struggling to keep up. It's like your old operating system is trying to run new software it wasn't designed for. That's why you feel so disoriented. That's why you feel like you're breaking. Because in a way, parts of your old self are breaking down to make way for something new. But that doesn't make it any less scary in the moment.

Here's what you need to focus on right now. Practical, grounding steps. Forget enlightenment for a minute. Forget spiritual growth. Your priority is stability, safety, and managing the intensity.

  • Get Grounded: This is probably the most important thing you can do. Kundalini can make you feel like you're floating, dissociated, or just completely out of your body. You need to pull yourself back down to earth.
    • Physical Grounding: Eat regular, simple, nourishing meals. Think root vegetables, whole grains, protein. Avoid sugar, caffeine, and alcohol - they'll just make things worse. Go for walks in nature, touch the earth, feel your feet on the ground. Take a warm bath. Do gentle stretching. Anything that brings your awareness back to your physical body.
    • Sensory Grounding: Focus on your five senses. What do you see? What do you hear? What do you smell? What do you taste? What do you feel? This pulls you out of your head and into the present moment. Carry something with a strong scent, like an essential oil, and smell it when you feel overwhelmed.
  • Seek Support (Carefully): You need someone to talk to, but not just anyone. You need someone who will listen without judgment, someone who won't tell you you're crazy, and ideally, someone who has some understanding of spiritual emergencies.
    • Therapist with a Spiritual Perspective: This is ideal. Look for therapists who specialize in "spiritual emergence" or "transpersonal psychology." They understand that not everything "weird" is pathology. This is where resources like Spiritual Emergency: When Personal Transformation Becomes a Crisis (paid link) can be really helpful. It’s a classic for a reason.
    • Trusted Friend or Family Member: If you have someone you trust completely, who can just listen and be present without trying to "fix" you or dismiss your experience, lean on them. Don't expect them to understand, just ask them to be there.
    • Online Communities: There are online forums and groups for people going through kundalini awakening. Be discerning, but finding others who share similar experiences can be incredibly validating.
  • Reduce Stimulation: Your nervous system is in overdrive. Cut back on screen time, loud music, chaotic environments, and stressful conversations. Create a calm, quiet space for yourself.
  • Simplify Your Life: You might not be able to handle your usual responsibilities. That's okay. Prioritize rest. Say no to commitments. Give yourself permission to just "be" with what's happening, as uncomfortable as it is.

Look, I know this sounds like a lot when you can barely get out of bed. But even one small step can make a difference. Just try one thing. Just try to eat something grounding. Just try to take a five-minute walk. Right? You're not trying to fix it all at once; you're just trying to manage the immediate intensity.

The Long Haul: Learning to Live with the Fire

This isn't a sprint; it's a marathon. Or maybe it's more like an involuntary ultra-marathon where you didn't train. The initial crisis phase can last weeks, months, sometimes even longer. But it does subside. The intensity ebbs and flows. You'll have good days and bad days. Don't get discouraged by the bad days. They're part of the process. It's like your system is recalibrating, and that takes time.

During this longer phase, you'll start to learn how to coexist with these energies. You'll learn what aggravates them and what soothes them. You'll develop your own coping mechanisms. This is where understanding what's happening on a deeper level can actually be helpful, not just terrifying.

  • Education is Your Friend: Reading about kundalini, even if it's scary, can help demystify the experience. It helps you realize you're not unique in this, and there's a body of knowledge around it. The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link) is another classic text that offers a medical and spiritual perspective. Also, When Spirit Leaps: Navigating the Process of Spiritual Awakening by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) and The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) are excellent resources. They offer practical advice and a sense of community, knowing others have gone through this.
  • Gentle Movement: Once the initial intensity calms a bit, gentle movement can be incredibly helpful. Think slow, mindful walking, restorative yoga, tai chi, qigong. Nothing strenuous. The idea is to help the energy move smoothly through your body, not to force it.
  • Creative Expression: This can be a powerful outlet. Journaling, drawing, painting, playing music, dancing - whatever allows you to express the overwhelming sensations and emotions without judgment. Kundalini often brings up old traumas and suppressed emotions; art can be a safe way to process them.
  • Mindfulness and Acceptance: This is hard, I know. When you're in the thick of it, acceptance feels impossible. But as the waves of energy come and go, try to observe them without getting swept away. "Okay, there's the buzzing again. There's the fear. I'm feeling it. It will pass." This isn't about liking it; it's about not fighting it so hard. Fighting it just creates more tension.

You're basically learning a new way of being. Your old normal is gone. And that's terrifying. But it also means you're being rebuilt, from the inside out. It's not a choice you made, but it's a journey you're on now. And you'll find your way through it. I promise you.

The Gift (Eventually): Finding Meaning in the Mayhem

Right now, "gift" probably feels like a sick joke. I get it. When I was going through it, if someone had told me this was a gift, I probably would've punched them. But truth is, once the dust settles - and it does settle - you'll start to see things differently. This whole terrifying experience, this involuntary awakening, it strips away everything that isn't real. It forces you to confront your fears, your traumas, your deepest beliefs. It breaks you open.

And what emerges from that breaking can be incredible. A deeper sense of self, an expanded consciousness, a deep empathy for others, a connection to something larger than yourself. You'll find a resilience you didn't know you had. You'll see the world with new eyes. You won't be the same person you were before the Reiki, before the kundalini hit. And honestly, you won't want to be. The person you become will be more authentic, more aware, more connected. Know what I mean? It's a brutal path to get there, but the destination... the destination is worth it.

So yeah, you're in the fire right now. It's hot, it's scary, and you feel like you're burning up. But you're not burning up; you're being forged. You're stronger than you think. Take it one breath at a time, one hour at a time, one day at a time. And remember, you're not alone in this dark night. I'm here, metaphorically speaking, pulling for you. You've got this. Just keep breathing. Keep grounding. And keep seeking that gentle support.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
